title: Multilingual Font Support — Custom Loader Demo

blocks.text: pdfnative-cli supports non-Latin scripts (Thai, Japanese, Arabic, Devanagari, etc.) via the --lang flag, which activates a programmatically registered font loader.

Font loaders for non-Latin scripts must be registered programmatically before building a PDF. Example: call registerFonts({ th: () => import('pdfnative/fonts/noto-thai-data.js') }) in a Node.js wrapper script, then call buildDocumentPDFBytes with fontEntries injected after awaiting loadFontData('th'). See 03-thai.js for a complete working example.

items: Thai, Japanese, Arabic, Devanagari — call registerFonts({ lang: () => import('pdfnative/fonts/noto-<lang>-data.js') })
Automatic script detection: pdfnative picks the correct registered font per text run

metadata.subject: Multilingual font support — loader registration pattern
metadata.keywords: multilang, fonts, thai, japanese, noto, loader
